        print(f"  id={n['id']} ({n['color']}): center={center}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

Wait, I think the player's movement is causing the scene to scroll or shift. The player is now at (26, 25.5) and there's a blue square at (21.5, 25.5) directly above. Let me try pressing SPACE to collect it.
Actually, looking more carefully:
- The white frame at (22, 25.5) with 16 pixels - this is a collected frame
- Blue square at (21.5, 25.5) - this is inside the white frame
- Player at (26, 25.5) - directly below the white frame
So the player is below the white frame which contains a blue square. The blue square at (21.5, 25.5) is inside the white frame at (22, 25.5). This might be the left blue square that was collected earlier.
But wait, there's also a blue square at (29.5, 21.5) which is the one I was trying to collect. And there's the gray bar at (29.5, 33.5).
Let me focus on collecting the blue square at (29.5, 21.5). The player is at (26, 25.5). I need to go DOWN and LEFT to get below it.
Actually, let me re-examine. The player is at (26, 25.5). The blue square at (29.5, 21.5) is to the left and below. Let me try going DOWN to get below the blue squares, then LEFT to align.
